12.title: Road to 1.0
12.description: Define and prove the 1.0 stability promise across our public surfaces
12.author: product-owner

13.title: Stability contract documentation
13.description: docs/stability.md + README tiering the public surfaces and what each promises through 1.0
13.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a squad user on 0.x, I want a written promise that my items reach 1.0 via sq migrate up, so that adopting squads before 1.0 is safe
As a script author, I want to know which CLI and --json surfaces are SemVer-stable, so that my automation survives upgrades
As an integrator, I want internals (Python import paths, generated .claude/ files) explicitly marked non-public, so that I don't build on the wrong layer

14.title: Project-level template and role overrides
14.description: squads/.templates/ overrides; lookup path and precedence join the durable contract, so it must land before 1.0
14.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a project lead, I want to override item templates from squads/.templates/, so that generated items follow our house format
As a project lead, I want to add or override role definitions for my project, so that the squad matches my actual team
As a maintainer upgrading squads, I want defined precedence and staleness behaviour for my overrides, so that an upgrade never silently breaks them
As a project lead, I want to supply each agent's name at init and at role creation, so that my squad's roster is named the way my team wants

15.title: Machine-readable surface audit
15.description: --json on every read command, documented exit codes, golden-file tests freezing the JSON shapes
15.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a script author, I want --json on every read command (blocked, mine, workload, inbox, check, refs included), so that I can parse squad state without scraping tables
As a CI pipeline author, I want documented exit codes, so that I can gate builds on commands like sq check
As a tool builder, I want the JSON shapes frozen by tests, so that an sq upgrade can't break my parser unannounced

16.title: Second backend: generic AGENTS.md
16.description: Prove the AgentBackend ABC with a cross-tool AGENTS.md implementation before the ABC freezes
16.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a team using a non-Claude agent tool, I want sq to generate an AGENTS.md, so that we can run squads with our own tooling
As a future backend implementer, I want a backend conformance test suite, so that I know exactly what the AgentBackend contract requires of me

17.title: 1.0 hardening
17.description: Migration fixture corpus in CI, ~1000-item scale sanity test, verified shell completion, decision on the Python >= 3.14 floor
17.author: product-owner

18.title: Backfill architecture documentation into squads
18.description: Put the architecture and the pre-bootstrap design decisions on the record as sq guides and ADRs, since the project started untracked
18.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a new agent or contributor, I want an architecture guide readable through sq, so that I understand the system's shape without spelunking through git history
As an architect, I want the standing design decisions on record as ADRs, so that future work can cite, build on, or formally revisit them
As a team member working an item, I want guides and ADRs cross-linked by refs, so that the relevant context travels with the work

19.title: Uniform item addressing: every command accepts both ID and number
19.description: Every ID-accepting surface (verbs, tree, --parent, ref add, search args) understands both the full ID (FEAT-000013) and the bare number (13), with one shared resolver
19.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a CLI user, I want to name an item by full ID or bare number in any command, so that one habit works everywhere
As a user copy-pasting an ID from a comment or tree, I want it accepted by every command including tree, --parent and ref add, so that handoffs never need manual reformatting
As a user who typos the type, I want sq to tell me 13 is a feature instead of silently obeying, so that I never act on the wrong item

20.title: Retype an item in place
20.description: sq <type> <n> retype <new-type>: fix a wrongly-filed item keeping its number, discussion and incoming refs
20.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a user who filed work under the wrong type, I want to retype it in place, so that the number, body and discussion survive the fix
As a teammate whose items reference the retyped one, I want every incoming ref, parent link and prose mention rewritten to the new ID, so that nothing dangles

11.title: sq check flags operator authors/assignees as unregistered
11.description: check() validates author/assignee against roles only, while the write gate accepts operators too
11.author: op-pierre

21.title: Slug arguments not validated: sq mine accepts unknown agents and silently defaults to manager
21.description: sq mine/inbox exit 0 for unknown slugs and bare sq mine defaults to manager; slug args should be required and validated against the roster across commands
21.author: op-pierre

1.title: Catherine Manager
1.description: Be the operator's first point of contact and run the work loop: understand the intent, delegate to the right specialists, integrate what they return, and drive each feature to done — keeping everything tracked in squads.
1.author: manager

extra.title: manager
extra.mission: Be the operator's first point of contact and run the work loop: understand the intent, delegate to the right specialists, integrate what they return, and drive each feature to done — keeping everything tracked in squads.
responsibilities: Triage incoming requests and clarify intent
Delegate work to the right specialist agents and integrate their results
Drive features through the loop (implement → review → fix) until done
Keep the backlog and statuses honest
extra.description: Default agent: triages the operator's request and routes it to the right specialist.

2.title: Robert Architect
2.description: Own the system's shape: design coherent solutions, record decisions as ADRs, and guide implementation.
2.author: architect

extra.title: architect

3.title: Olivia Lead
3.description: Turn features into well-scoped tasks, sequence the work, and unblock the team.
3.author: tech-lead

extra.title: tech lead
extra.mission: Turn features into well-scoped tasks, sequence the work, and unblock the team.
responsibilities: Author tasks (`sq create task`); set each task's parent to the feature it implements
Map each subtask to a single user story (`sq task <n> add-subtask "…" --story USn`)
For a bug fix or review follow-up, link via refs (`sq task <n> ref add <id> --kind fixes|addresses`)
Sequence and assign work; unblock developers
extra.description: Coordination and breaking features into tasks.

4.description: Guard quality: review changes critically, request changes when needed, approve when sound.

extra.mission: Guard quality: review changes critically, request changes when needed, approve when sound.
responsibilities: Review diffs for correctness and clarity
Drive code-review items to a verdict

6.title: Hugo Ops
6.description: Keep delivery smooth: maintain CI/CD, infrastructure, and the release process.

7.title: Nina Product
7.description: Represent the user: capture requirements as features and user stories, prioritise the backlog.

extra.title: product owner

responsibilities: Write each feature's user stories (`sq story add`)
Prioritise the backlog and define acceptance criteria

22.title: Counter can regress, allowing sequence-number reuse after an item file disappears
22.description: Hand-edited or repair-rebuilt indexes accept a regressed counter, so deleted items' numbers get reused; repair sets counter to max-found, ignoring the previous high-water mark
22.author: product-owner

23.title: Sanctioned item removal
23.description: sq <type> <n> remove: a safe, first-class way to delete or retire an item — today the only option is manual file surgery plus hand-editing the index
23.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As an operator who created an item by mistake, I want sq remove to take it off the books safely, so that I never have to hand-edit files or the index
As a teammate whose items reference the removed one, I want removal to refuse or cleanly sever those refs, so that nothing dangles silently
As someone auditing a squad later, I want number gaps to be explainable, so that a missing sequence number reads as a recorded removal, not corruption

24.title: Operation reflog
24.description: An append-only JSONL log of every mutating sq operation (who, when, what, before/after), with a sq reflog command to read it
24.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As an operator, I want a chronological log of every mutation with its actor, so that I can review what the agents did without having been in their conversations
As a team member investigating an anomaly, I want removals, retypes and forced transitions explainable from the squad directory alone, so that a gap or surprise reads as history, not corruption
As a tool builder, I want the reflog as stable, documented JSONL, so that I can build dashboards and automation on the operation stream

25.title: show prints a redundant 'Body' literal before the body
25.description: sq <type> <n> show prefixes the body with a bare 'Body' label; the body is self-evident after the metadata panel and usually opens with its own headings
25.author: op-pierre

26.title: Rendered show output
26.description: sq show renders body and discussion as styled markdown on a TTY (headings, bullets, code), with --raw opt-out, NO_COLOR respected, and plain output when piped
26.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As an operator reading the backlog in my terminal, I want show to render the markdown, so that sq tree + sq show covers browsing and reading without external viewers
As an agent or script consuming show output, I want piped/--raw/NO_COLOR output plain and stable, so that rendering never breaks my parsing
As a user with an ID or number in hand, I want a root sq show command that displays any item regardless of type, so that I can read anything in one step without naming its type
As a reader of a feature, task or review, I want show to include the sub-entity summary table and the discussion, so that one command gives me the whole item, not just its body
As a reader wanting the whole story, I want --full to render each sub-entity and its comments in tidy panes along with the main discussion, so that one command reads as the item's dossier

As an agent briefing on an item, I want the squads skill and the sq-<type> skills to teach reading with --full --comments as the standard briefing move, so that decisions captured in discussion comments are never missed

27.title: Explicit ID padding with a raise-padding migration
27.description: padding: 6 stored in the index; create errors when the counter would overflow it; a one-way repad command renames all files to the new width; ID parsing tolerant of any width since file contents are never rewritten
27.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a squad owner whose counter hits the cap, I want create to fail with a clear index-full error naming the fix, so that the format never silently grows a digit
As a squad owner raising the padding, I want one command that renames every file to the new width and rebuilds the index, so that the squad stays uniform without hand-work
As a teammate whose items hold old-width refs and mentions, I want ID parsing tolerant of any padding, so that content written before the raise keeps resolving forever

28.title: sq ui — a terminal UI for browsing the squad
28.description: An sq ui command opening a TUI: navigate the tree, read items with tabs for stories/body/discussion; browse-first, mutations a later increment
28.author: product-owner

29.title: sq web — a local web view of the squad
29.description: An sq web command starting a local webserver to browse the squad in a browser; read-only first, with full CRUD as a possible long-term destination
29.author: product-owner

30.title: tree: priority and title separated by a bare space
30.description: In sq tree, '🟠 high Stability contract documentation' runs together — put a middle dot between priority and title
30.author: op-pierre

31.title: Squads backend
31.description: Umbrella for technical work on the sq engine itself — services, index, models, workflow, rendering, agent-backend plumbing — as opposed to product surfaces like the CLI grammar, TUI or web
31.author: product-owner

32.title: Pluggable index store: SQLite / SQL databases behind the index
32.description: Abstract the index behind a store interface and offer SQLite (and real databases via SQLAlchemy) as alternatives to .squads.json — the .md files stay the only source of truth
32.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a team running many agents in parallel, I want the index behind real transactions, so that concurrent sq calls stop serializing on one file lock
As an owner of a large squad, I want an indexed store keeping list/tree/search fast at thousands of items, so that scale doesn't degrade the daily loop
As a user choosing a store, I want switching to be config plus sq repair — reversible both ways, so that the .md files remain the only thing I must never lose

33.title: Remote mode: the sq CLI as a client to a squads server
33.description: A mode switch in .squads.toml (offline default / remote): in remote mode every command calls remote endpoints with full feature-and-flag parity, through a Protocol-typed service interface the CLI depends on
33.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a maintainer of the codebase, I want the CLI decoupled from the concrete Service behind Python Protocols, so that plugging a different service implementation (remote or otherwise) is possible without touching the commands
As an operator or agent on a remote squad, I want every offline command and flag to work identically in remote mode, so that the transport never changes how I work
As a squad owner, I want the mode set in .squads.toml with offline as the default, so that remote is an explicit per-squad choice and existing squads are untouched

34.title: Async to the core: the service and IO go async end-to-end
34.description: Make the service layer, index store and file IO async (anyio or similar) all the way down, with the sync bridge only at the CLI entry edge — so the protocol seam, TUI, web server and remote client are all async-native
34.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a developer of an async consumer (TUI, web server, remote client), I want the service async-native, so that I never wrap calls in executor threads
As a CLI user, I want the async conversion invisible, so that every command behaves and outputs byte-identically to today

35.title: Typed ref vocabulary: validated kinds, optional by design
35.description: ref add validates --kind against the documented vocabulary (typos rejected, check flags unknowns); untyped refs stay first-class for plain cross-reference; one canonical kinds table in the docs
35.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a user adding a typed ref, I want a typo'd kind rejected on the spot with the valid vocabulary, so that I can't silently create an edge nothing consumes
As a user linking context, I want plain untyped refs to stay first-class, so that not every link needs a taxonomy decision
As a team member learning the system, I want one documented table of kinds with direction and consumers, so that I pick the right kind without archaeology across five docs
As a user drafting an item that needs another one first, I want to record depends-on from the item I'm editing, so that dependencies are authorable without touching the blocker

36.title: Type aliases in the CLI grammar
36.description: Short and single-letter aliases for the item-type commands (sq feat / sq f, sq t, sq b, ...) — hidden from help clutter, documented once, frozen with the grammar
36.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a daily CLI user, I want sq f / sq t / sq b to work everywhere the full type word does, so that the commands I type all day are as short as they are unambiguous
As a newcomer reading help and docs, I want aliases out of the command list but documented in one table, so that discoverability doesn't cost clarity

37.title: Ref graph view: sq graph <item>
37.description: An ego-centric graph command: walk the ref edges from one item to a given depth, filtered by kind and direction, rendered as a tree — plus dot/mermaid export for the full picture
37.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As an operator sequencing work, I want the dependency tree of one item to a chosen depth, so that I can see what it waits on and what waits on it before greenlighting
As an agent briefing on an item, I want to filter the graph by kind and direction, so that I pull only the context that matters for my job
As a user wanting the big picture, I want a dot/mermaid export, so that the full graph renders in tools made for it instead of flooding my terminal

38.title: CLI frontend
38.description: The daily terminal experience of sq: complete and rendered reading, graph navigation, and the ergonomics of driving a squad from the command line
38.author: product-owner

39.title: Tree filters
39.description: sq tree gains the same filters as list (--status, --priority, --assignee, --type) plus --depth; matches keep their ancestor path so the tree stays a tree
39.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As an operator reviewing the board, I want to filter the tree by status, priority, assignee or type, so that greenlight discussions look at exactly the slice that matters
As a reader of a deep backlog, I want --depth and context-preserving pruning, so that a filtered tree stays readable and every match keeps its place in the hierarchy

40.title: Dual-regime role definitions: subagent invocation vs live conversation
40.description: Bundled roles' working agreements split by regime: spawned-for-a-job (record everything before you vanish) vs live with the operator (judgment on when handoffs become true) — fixing the rigidity that produces false inbox signals
40.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As an agent spawned for a job, I want my role to tell me exactly what must be on the record before I return, so that the loop never loses my work when my chat evaporates
As an agent working live with the operator, I want agreements that separate recording decisions from signalling handoffs, so that I never put a false call-to-action in a teammate's inbox
As a teammate reading my inbox, I want every @mention to be a real, current call-to-action, so that I can trust it as my work queue

41.title: Onboard through the CLI: roles readable via sq, not the filesystem
41.description: sq role show displays the complete definition (working agreements included) and every onboarding text (CLAUDE.md section, skills) directs agents to sq commands instead of reading files under squads/
41.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As an agent adopting my persona, I want sq role show to give me the complete definition including working agreements, so that I never open the file to learn my job
As an agent following the onboarding texts, I want every read they prescribe to be an sq command, so that one interface covers work and identity — locally and, someday, remotely

42.title: Fix counter regression and sequence-number reuse (BUG-000022)
42.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Repair keeps high-water mark: counter = max(previous counter, max found on disk)
Load-time counter validation: repair upward when stored counter is below max item sequence
Report items missing from disk vs the previous index in repair/check
Regression tests (service + CLI) covering both regression routes

43.title: Validate slug arguments across mine/inbox and slug-accepting surfaces (BUG-000021)
43.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Add resolve_slug_or_raise helper to _cli/_common.py
Make sq mine require slug argument
Validate slug on inbox, workload, comment --as, update --assignee/--author
Add service-level and CLI smoke tests

44.title: Cosmetic cleanup: drop redundant show 'Body' label and add tree separator (BUG-000025, BUG-000030)
44.author: tech-lead

45.title: Clear pre-existing lint/format debt on the release branch (F401 + format drift)
45.author: tech-lead

46.title: Shared item-address resolver + typed-surface type enforcement
46.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Shared resolver: typed (live-DB type check) + type-less variants, both-forms unknown-item errors
Close the silent type-mismatch hole on the item-verb surface (13 is FEAT-000013, not a task)

47.title: Adopt the shared resolver across all ID-accepting surfaces + uniform error wording
47.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Wire tree root, --parent, --ref, ref add/rm, list --parent, role/skill/operator targets, subtask --story through the resolver
Uniform unknown-item error wording mentioning both accepted forms everywhere

Type-mismatch wording is inconsistent on typed surfaces depending on input form. For the SAME item, 'sq task 2 show' errors '2 is FEAT-000002 (feature), not a task' (names actual item+type, matches the recorded decision) but 'sq task FEAT-000002 show' errors 'FEAT-000002 is not a task (expected TASK-…)' — it never tells the user FEAT-000002 IS a feature. resolve_item_id_typed short-circuits the full-ID branch on the prefix string check (_common.py ~line 280) before the DB lookup, reusing the old wording. Acceptance requires wrong-type errors 'consistently everywhere, naming the actual item and type'. Fix: in the full-ID branch, when prefix mismatches, do the DB lookup and emit the same 'X is <id> (<type>), not a <type>' wording as the bare-number branch.

Unknown-item error wording drifts across the four resolver branches; acceptance demands 'both accepted forms, consistently everywhere'. Variants observed: (a) typed bare: 'no item with number 999 (use TASK-000999 or bare 999)'; (b) any bare: 'no item with number 999 (use a bare number like 999 or a full ID like TYPE-000999)'; (c) any full-ID: 'no item with number 999 (tried FEAT-000999 or bare 999)' — this one names neither form as a 'form' and omits the full-ID guidance; (d) typed full-ID uses the command prefix while any uses literal 'TYPE-'. Pick one canonical phrasing for 'mentions both forms' and use it in all four branches of resolve_item_id_typed/_any in _common.py.

Old resolve_item_id (_common.py:231) is now dead production code — no production caller remains after _items.py switched its verb callback to resolve_item_id_typed (only its own test_resolve_item_id references it). The two new resolvers re-implement the same lexical prefix/rpartition munging internally, so the lexical phase is now written three times. Either delete resolve_item_id (and fold its lexical assertions into the typed test) or have the new resolvers delegate to it for the lexical phase. Quality/maintainability, not correctness.

Minor grammar in type-mismatch messages: 'not a operator' / 'not a epic' (a/an agreement) — produced for OPERATOR and EPIC types. The recorded example is 'not a task' so it reads fine for most types but not vowel-initial ones. Low cosmetic; can be folded with the F1/F2 wording cleanup.

49.title: Ref-kind vocabulary is closed in 1.0, with the extension point reserved for post-1.0
49.author: architect

50.title: Ref-kind vocabulary constant + validation at all authoring entry points
50.author: tech-lead

Keep bare ref add first-class: default related validates trivially, no nudge, no warning
Wire depends-on/supersedes/duplicates as accepted kinds at ref add and create --ref id:kind; authorable from the dependent

51.title: Ref-kind consumers + canonical kinds table in docs
51.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Single canonical eight-row kinds table in workflow.md.j2 (meaning/direction/consumer); ref add --kind help points at it
depends-on equivalence in sq blocked (A depends-on B == B blocks A; mixed usage works)

Style: _check_decisions() in _maintenance.py uses a function-local 'from squads._models._enums import Status' (line ~332) when ItemType is already imported from that same module at the top. Hoist Status to the module-level import for consistency. Non-blocking — no cycle, gates clean.

53.title: Split bundled role working agreements into spawned vs live regimes
53.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Restructure role.md.j2 working agreements into the two explicit regimes with the shared 'record when it becomes true' principle; sq sync propagates to all 8 roles + dev pool

In the live-regime text, separate recording a decision (--as, when made) from signalling a handoff (@mention, only when work moves); reference the squads skill's operator section instead of duplicating it

Consistency pass on _interactions.py 'For <role>' handoff lines so each carries its trigger condition (e.g. 'when the feature is greenlit, @tech-lead'), keeping every @mention a real call-to-action; align greeting/squads skill cross-references

54.title: sq role show renders the complete role definition (card + item body)
54.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Render active role item body (working agreements + skills) below the catalog card in sq role show, with a test asserting the agreements are present

55.title: Onboarding texts direct to sq commands, not squads/ file paths
55.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Rewrite the CLAUDE.md impersonation paragraph to direct to sq role show; verify the rest of the generated onboarding text is path-free and sync propagates

56.title: Comment text containing literal sq marker syntax breaks the item's marker integrity
56.author: manager

57.title: Guard all prose inputs against literal sq marker syntax (comments, sub-entity titles)
57.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Add one shared marker-syntax guard; apply to comment messages and sub-entity titles (create + update)
Tests: each newly-guarded path rejects marker tags (incl. backtick-wrapped); item title/description false-positive guard; body behaviour unchanged

58.title: Rendered show: markdown body, comments facet, scope flag, root sq show
58.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Render body as styled markdown on a TTY
Plain/byte-stable output when piped, --raw, or NO_COLOR; --json unchanged
Root sq show <id|number> for any work-item type
Default summary table + --comments main discussion panes

59.title: Rendered show dossier: --full sub-entity panes with embedded comments
59.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Sub-entity panes and the --full --comments dossier

60.title: Teach show --full --comments in generated skills and onboarding (US6)
60.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: Teach --full --comments in generated skills and onboarding
Plain-output escaping leak: sub-entity pane titles double-escaped in piped/NO_COLOR/--raw path
Nit: badge/column duplication between _common.py and _discussion.py
Nit: test gap — no assertion for literal-bracket fidelity in plain pane titles

62.title: Comment-scoping convention: sub-entity discussions vs main discussion
62.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As an agent writing a comment, I want the skills I load to tell me whether to use `sq <type> <n> <kind> <k> comment` or `sq <type> <n> comment`, so that I never route fix rationale or acceptance notes to the wrong discussion

As an agent reading a dossier with `--full --comments`, I want each sub-entity pane to contain the discussion that is scoped to it, so that I can brief on a finding or story without hunting through the parent item's main discussion

As an agent reading my inbox, I want @mentions placed in sub-entity discussions to reach me just as reliably as item-level mentions, so that I can trust the inbox regardless of where in the item the mention was written

63.title: Teach sub-entity comment scoping in the generated skills + role templates
63.author: tech-lead

subentities.title: State the convention once in squads_skill.md.j2 (single source): sub-entity command for scoped material, main command for cross-cutting, with one example per sub-entity type; add the brief scoping-principle sentence + reference to role.md.j2 working agreements (both regimes)

Add role-specific scoped-comment guidance to the per-role lines in _interactions.py — REVIEW reviewer/dev (finding-scoped), FEATURE po/tech-lead (story-scoped), TASK dev (subtask-scoped) — each pointing at the squads-skill convention, not restating it; verify sq sync regenerates every skill + role file with markers intact

Tests: assert generated squads/sq-review/sq-feature/sq-task skills + role files carry the convention/guidance, and a no-regression assertion that an @mention in a sub-entity discussion is surfaced by sq inbox; keep pyright + ruff clean

64.title: Align agent-type command groups with the item CLI grammar
64.description: Bring role/skill/operator command groups in line with the item grammar: item-first addressing, styled body rendering, and a single list surface
64.author: product-owner

subentities.title: As a CLI user, I want to address roles, skills, and operators with the same item-first grammar I use for every other type (sq role N show, sq skill N show, sq operator N show), so that one habit works everywhere without exceptions

As a CLI user reading a role or skill definition, I want the body rendered as styled markdown (headings, bullets, code blocks, panes) just like any other item show, so that role bodies are as readable as feature bodies

As a CLI user, I want a single list surface for tracked items (sq list -t role|skill|operator) and a dedicated catalog command for the bundled role catalog (sq role catalog), so that the command surface is clean and unambiguous

As the stability contract author, I want this feature's grammar decisions recorded as a deferral obligation on FEAT-000013 before it closes, so that the contract document reflects the final CLI surface accurately

65.title: Flip role/skill/operator to item-first grammar; add sq role catalog; remove the list subcommands

67.title: FEAT-000064: negative-path error UX leaks the internal _addr subgroup and fails 'error comprehensibly'
67.description: AddressDispatchGroup routes every unknown token to the hidden _addr subgroup; removed/typo'd/old-grammar invocations produce baffling errors that leak _addr and omit the documented migration hint.
67.author: reviewer

subentities.title: Removed list commands do not error comprehensibly (US3 acceptance)
The hidden _addr name leaks into every negative-path usage/error line
show/regen/rm are undiscoverable from sq role / --help (US1 spirit)

N2 resolver duplication: resolve_agent_addr iterates db.items inline instead of reusing item helpers
N3 empty-body hint inaccurate: suggests a 'body' verb the groups do not have
N4 inline shape check: _resolve_addr reimplements full-ID detection instead of _is_full_id_shape
N5 stale comment: SKIL-000002 should be SKILL-000002 in test_skill_item_first_grammar
N6 test coverage: no direct test for REV-000061 bracket/no-backslash fidelity on the new body path
N7 sq dev consistency: still uses the old listing shape, reads inconsistently against the flipped groups

71.title: Frontmatter is the source of truth; the index is rebuildable
71.description: Item state lives in .md frontmatter; .squads.json is a derived index that sq repair reconstructs
71.author: architect

72.title: One global monotonic ID counter across all item types
72.description: A single counter makes every ID number globally unique; allocation only inside the index transaction
72.author: architect

73.title: Forward-only refs with computed backrefs
73.description: Items store outgoing refs with kind inline; backrefs are computed by inversion, never persisted
73.author: architect

74.title: Marker-safe editing: sq owns the anchor regions, agents own the prose
74.description: File content is mutated only through the sections layer; anchor tags delimit managed regions; agent prose is never rewritten
74.author: architect

75.title: Pluggable backends behind the AgentBackend ABC; .claude/ is pointers
75.description: Harnesses sit behind an ABC + registry; real content lives under the squad folder, .claude/ holds only pointers
75.author: architect

76.title: Dotted 0.x schema-version scheme with the sq migrate runner
76.description: Schema version names the release that introduced it, compared as a tuple; upgrades run through ordered sq migrate steps
76.author: architect

77.title: Time is injectable through a single clock module
77.description: All timestamps route through _clock.now()/iso(); freezable in tests and forgeable via --at for history-preserving adoption
77.author: architect

78.title: Module-privacy convention: underscore-prefixed internals, no re-exporting inits
78.description: Every implementation module is private with leading underscores and non-re-exporting inits, so 1.0 freezes no accidental public API
78.author: architect

79.title: squads architecture: layering, data model, and the marker mechanism
79.description: A standalone-readable map of how squads is built: the cli to services to index/backends/rendering layering, the item/sub-entity/index data model, and the marker mechanism — pointing at sq docs internals for depth and citing the standing ADRs.
79.author: architect

80.title: Inbox surfaces stale mentions from Accepted decisions and Published guides
80.author: manager

81.title: Add ACCEPTED and PUBLISHED to the TERMINAL status set
81.author: manager
